site.title: Upskill Fleet Academy
site.heroHeadline: Build Real-World Skills in AI Security, Cybersecurity & Cloud Security
site.heroSubheading: Expert-led, hands-on training designed for students, security professionals, cloud engineers, and technology leaders looking to advance their careers.
site.heroSupporting: Learn through practical labs, real-world case studies, threat simulations, and industry best practices used by modern enterprises.
site.seoDescription: Upskill Fleet Academy — Expert-led programs in AI Security, Cyber Security, Cloud Security, DevSecOps, and Financial Markets & Management. Practical labs, weekend cohorts, and career-oriented learning for students and professionals.
site.scheduleNote: Weekends only · live online. Master classes are 1 hour each (weekends 1–3 from 21 June 2026). Bootcamp is 12 hours total — 3 hours per weekend over four weekends.
site.aboutIntro: Learn from experienced practitioners across security, cloud, AI, and financial markets—focused on real-world skills and career outcomes.
site.coursesIntro: Three learning paths—AI & Cyber Security, AWS Cloud Computing, and Financial Markets & Management (FMM)—each structured from beginner to advanced.
site.guestFacultyIntro: Our programs are supported by experienced professionals from security, cloud, AI, and financial services domains, bringing diverse industry perspectives into the classroom.
site.testimonialsIntro: Learner experiences from our AI & Cyber Security, AWS Cloud, and Financial Markets programs.

courses.summary: Build practical AI security and cyber defense skills—from LLM fundamentals and DevSecOps-style controls to supply chain risk and AI governance.
courses.format: Workshop · Hands-on Labs · Threat Scenarios · Case Studies

topics: Cyber security fundamentals & zero trust basics
AI attacks and defenses using DevSecOps
Prompt injection defense & LLM threat modeling

topics: AWS global infrastructure & core service models (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS)
IAM — users, roles, policies & least privilege
Amazon EC2 — compute fundamentals & instance types
AWS security best practices & Well-Architected Framework

courses.summary: Understand financial markets, investment principles, and industry fundamentals through practical learning and certification-focused guidance.
courses.idealFor: College students, beginners in finance, aspiring investors, and working professionals exploring financial markets
courses.format: Interactive Sessions · Practical Examples · Certification Guidance

sessions.topic: Master Class — Weekend 1: AI Security & Cyber Security (orientation, threats, and zero-trust basics)
Master Class — Weekend 2: AWS Cloud Computing (cloud fundamentals, IAM & EC2 overview)
Master Class — Weekend 3: Financial Markets & Management (NISM & NCFM pathways)
Bootcamp — Weekend 4: Full-module kickoff & shared foundations (all three learning paths)
Bootcamp — Weekend 5: AI Security & Cyber Security (labs, threat modeling, incident playbooks)
Bootcamp — Weekend 6: AWS Cloud Computing (IAM, EC2, VPC & networking labs)
Bootcamp — Weekend 7: Financial Markets capstone & integrated review

curriculum.description: Three focused weekend sessions introduce AI/Cyber, AWS Cloud, and Financial Markets tracks before the intensive bootcamp.
milestones: Orientation to labs and cohort tools

curriculum.description: Four weekends of applied learning—3 hours each—for hands-on skills across security, cloud, AI, and financial markets.
Build portfolio-ready skills, certification readiness, and confidence to advance in security, cloud, or financial markets roles.

testimonials.quote: The AI security labs turned abstract risks into skills I could use immediately. Threat modeling for LLMs went from intimidating to practical—I applied the framework the same week.
I started with almost no cloud background. The AWS path made IAM, EC2, and networking build on each other naturally. The hands-on exercises felt like real work, not theory.
Financial markets finally clicked. NISM concepts, market orders, and risk were explained clearly—exactly what I needed as someone new to investing.

faq.a: Students and professionals pursuing careers in AI security, cybersecurity, AWS cloud computing, or financial markets. The AI & Cyber and AWS tracks suit engineers and security practitioners; the FMM track suits learners preparing for NISM/NCFM and investment fundamentals.

faq.q: When and how are sessions delivered?
faq.a: The current cohort runs on weekends only, live online, starting 21 June 2026. The first three weekends are 1-hour master classes (one per focus area). Weekends four through seven are a 12-hour bootcamp—3 hours each weekend—covering the full module.

faq.q: Are technology and finance tracks separate?
faq.a: Yes. AI & Cyber Security and AWS Cloud Computing are technology & security programs. Financial Markets & Management (FMM) is a separate track with its own instructor and certification-oriented content.
Recordings are provided where policy allows; links will be added under Resources after each cohort.

faq.q: Do I need cloud accounts for labs?
faq.a: Most labs use open-source tools you can run locally or in our guided lab setup—no paid cloud subscription is required to start. When a session needs cloud resources (for example AWS Cloud Computing labs), we will say so in advance; you may create an AWS free-tier account only if required for that lab. If you use your own cloud account or company environment, follow your organization's policies and obtain admin approval where needed.

footer.tagline: Practical learning. Industry experience. Career growth.
footer.closing: Whether you're starting your journey in technology or advancing into specialized security or financial markets roles, our programs are designed to help you build skills that matter in today's AI-driven world.
